indication: Daratumumab in combination with bortezomib and dexamethasone is recommended for use within the Cancer Drugs Fund as an option for treating relapsed multiple myeloma in people who have had 1 previous treatment. This weekly bortezomib regimen is for patients who experience neuropathy or those with pre-existing neuropathy.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Cycles 9 and 10 only. Weekly bortezomib continuation phase (cycles 9 onwards). Both twice-weekly and weekly regimens include 32 doses total; bortezomib continues to end of cycle 10 in weekly regimen. Dose banded per national dose bands (2.5).

Cycles 9 and 10: day 1 only. Cycle 11 onwards: day 1 only (daratumumab monotherapy). Prescribed per national dose bands (20 NS). Continue until disease progression. Use PES filter (0.22 or 0.2 micrometre). Rapid infusion protocol available from 3rd infusion onwards if criteria met (90 min total). Inform blood transfusion before first administration. Daratumumab binds CD38 on RBCs - patient requires red cell phenotyping.

Cycles 9 and 10: 20mg day 1 (pre-daratumumab, reduce to 10mg in over 75s). Can be given as IV bolus equivalent. Additional dexamethasone 20mg oral on days 2, 8, 9, 15, 16, 22, 23 (reduce to 10mg in over 75s). Cycle 11 onwards: 20mg day 1 pre-daratumumab; 4mg days 2 and 3 to reduce delayed infusion reactions.

pre_medications.indication: Pre-medication for daratumumab infusion related reaction prevention
Pre-medication for daratumumab infusion related reaction prevention. Max 4000mg/24 hours.

take_home_medicines.days: Days 2, 8, 9, 15, 16, 22, 23 (cycles 9 and 10); day 1 of next cycle
take_home_medicines.special_instructions: Reduce to 10mg in patients over 75 years old. Take in the morning with or after food. Dispense all days on day 1 of cycle. Cycle 11 onwards: 4mg days 2 and 3 only.
VZV prophylaxis. Supply 28 days or original pack.

take_home_medicines.days: Monday, Wednesday and Friday for 28 days
take_home_medicines.special_instructions: PCP prophylaxis. May be dispensed as 480mg twice a day on Mon/Wed/Fri.

For patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed. Options include esomeprazole 20mg OD, omeprazole 20mg OD, lansoprazole 15mg OD, pantoprazole 20mg OD, rabeprazole 20mg OD, cimetidine 400mg BD, famotidine 20mg OD, nizatidine 150mg BD, ranitidine 150mg BD.

dose_modifications.condition: Less than 0.5x10⁹/L or febrile neutropenia (fever ≥38.5°C and neutrophils less than 1)
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt daratumumab and monitor FBC weekly. Once neutrophils recover to 1x10⁹/L, resume daratumumab at 16mg/kg. Bortezomib: consider treatment delay or dose reduction or growth factor support. Seek consultant advice. No dose reductions of daratumumab are recommended.
Interrupt daratumumab and monitor FBC weekly. Once platelets recover to 50x10⁹/L or greater, resume daratumumab at 16mg/kg.
Consider treatment delay or dose reduction or platelet transfusion. Seek consultant advice.
Initiate bortezomib at 0.7mg/m². May be escalated to 1mg/m² or reduced to 0.5mg/m² in subsequent cycles based on tolerability. Bilirubin 1.5xULN or below: 100% dose.
No formal studies conducted. Based on population PK analysis no dosage adjustments necessary.
100% dose.
Clinical decision required.
Continue full dose bortezomib.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 1 with pain or grade 2
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce bortezomib dose to 1mg/m².

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 2 with pain or grade 3
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue until resolved to grade 1 or less, then reinitiate at 0.7mg/m².

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 4 or severe autonomic neuropathy
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue bortezomib permanently.
Discontinue until grade 1 or below. First occurrence: reinitiate at 1mg/m². Second occurrence: reduce to 0.7mg/m². If recurs at lowest dose, discontinue unless benefit outweighs risk.
Once symptoms resolve, resume at no more than half the rate. If no further reaction, rate escalation may resume.
If decreases to ≤grade 2, restart at no more than half the rate. Permanently discontinue upon third occurrence of grade 3 or greater.
Permanently discontinue treatment.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: To initiate a new cycle: neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L and platelets ≥50x10⁹/L unless low counts due to bone marrow infiltration with myeloma, in which case may proceed at consultant discretion with blood products and growth factor support.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, U&Es, Ca²⁺ and LFTs prior to day 1 of each cycle
Paraprotein and/or light chains prior to each cycle
Hepatitis B virus (HBV) testing before initiating daratumumab; hepatitis C, CMV and HIV if clinically appropriate
Red cell phenotyping required before first daratumumab - send blood sample to transfusion lab
Regular blood glucose monitoring (dexamethasone)
Vital signs before infusion start, every 15 minutes during first 60 minutes, and at end of each daratumumab infusion
For rapid rate infusion: observe 30 minutes post-completion for delayed IRR
special_warnings: Continuation/maintenance phase: cycle length changes from 21 days to 28 days from cycle 9 onwards
Cycles 9 and 10 include bortezomib; cycle 11 onwards is daratumumab plus dexamethasone only
Ensure correct daratumumab cycle 9 onwards protocol is selected
Daratumumab interferes with indirect antiglobulin tests (binds CD38 on RBCs) - notify blood transfusion before first dose
Daratumumab may cause false positive SPE and IFE assay results for IgG kappa myeloma protein
VZV prophylaxis with aciclovir 400mg twice daily mandatory
Rapid infusion protocol (90 min) available from 3rd infusion if criteria met - unlicensed rate
Dexamethasone doses before and after daratumumab are to reduce IRR risk AND serve as steroid component of triple combination

drugs.notes: day 1 of each cycle | Cycles 9 and 10: 20mg day 1 (pre-daratumumab, reduce to 10mg in over 75s). Can be given as IV bolus equivalent. Additional dexamethasone 20mg oral on days 2, 8, 9, 15, 16, 22, 23 (reduce to 10mg in over 75s). Cycle 11 onwards: 20mg day 1 pre-daratumumab; 4mg days 2 and 3 to reduce delayed infusion reactions.

protocol_name: DVd SC (weekly) Bortezomib Daratumumab SC Dexamethasone Cycles 9 onwards
indication: Daratumumab in combination with bortezomib and dexamethasone is recommended for use as an option for treating relapsed multiple myeloma in people who have had 1 previous treatment. This weekly bortezomib regimen is for patients who experience neuropathy or those with pre-existing neuropathy.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Cycles 9 and 10 only. Continuation phase (cycles 9 onwards). Bortezomib continues to end of cycle 10 in weekly regimen. Both twice-weekly and weekly regimens include 32 doses of bortezomib.

Subcutaneous injection 1800mg over 3-5 minutes. Cycles 9 and 10: day 1 only. Cycle 11 onwards: day 1 only. Inject into subcutaneous tissue of abdomen 7.5cm right or left of navel. Do not inject into other sites. Rotate injection sites. Do not inject into red, bruised, tender or scarred skin. Continue until disease progression. Patients require patient identification card for 6 months after stopping. DARZALEX SC formulation. Post-injection monitoring required.

Cycles 9 and 10: 20mg day 1 (pre-daratumumab; can be IV bolus equivalent; reduce to 10mg in over 75s). Additional 20mg oral days 2, 8, 9, 15, 16, 22, 23 (reduce to 10mg in over 75s). Cycle 11 onwards: 20mg day 1; 4mg days 2 and 3 to reduce delayed injection reactions.

pre_medications.drug_name: Antihistamine (chlorphenamine 4mg oral or loratadine 10mg or cetirizine 10mg or fexofenadine 120mg or acrivastine 8mg)
pre_medications.indication: Pre-medication for daratumumab injection related reaction prevention
Pre-medication for daratumumab injection related reaction prevention. Max 4000mg/24 hours.

take_home_medicines.days: Days 2, 8, 9, 15, 16, 22, 23 (cycles 9 and 10); day 1 of next cycle
take_home_medicines.special_instructions: Reduce to 10mg in patients over 75 years old. Take morning with or after food. Cycle 11 onwards: 4mg days 2 and 3 only. Dispense 1 dose day 1 of next cycle to take on morning of next daratumumab injection.
Supply 1 x OP to cover all cycles. Options: chlorphenamine 4mg, loratadine 10mg, cetirizine 10mg, fexofenadine 120mg, or acrivastine 8mg.
VZV prophylaxis. Supply 28 days or original pack.

take_home_medicines.days: Monday, Wednesday and Friday for 28 days

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 1mg/m².

dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue until grade 1 or less, reinitiate at 0.7mg/m².

Discontinue DARZALEX therapy immediately and permanently. Approximately 11% of patients experience IRR with SC daratumumab.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: To initiate a new cycle: neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L and platelets ≥50x10⁹/L unless low counts due to bone marrow infiltration, in which case may proceed at consultant discretion.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, U&Es, Ca²⁺ and LFTs prior to day 1 of each cycle
Paraprotein and/or light chains prior to each cycle
Hepatitis B virus (HBV) testing before initiating daratumumab
Red cell phenotyping required before first daratumumab
Regular blood glucose monitoring (dexamethasone)
Observe for 4 hours following first daratumumab SC dose
For patients with COPD history: consider post-injection bronchodilators and inhaled corticosteroids
special_warnings: Continuation/maintenance phase: cycle length changes from 21 days to 28 days from cycle 9 onwards
Cycles 9 and 10 include bortezomib; cycle 11 onwards is daratumumab SC plus dexamethasone only
DARZALEX subcutaneous formulation - fixed dose 1800mg; do not administer at other body sites
Daratumumab SC can cause severe/serious IRRs including anaphylaxis - median onset 3.7 hours
Oral corticosteroids must be administered to all patients following SC daratumumab injection to reduce delayed IRR risk
Daratumumab binds CD38 on RBCs - positive indirect antiglobulin (Coombs) test may persist up to 6 months
Daratumumab may cause false positive SPE/IFE assay results
VZV prophylaxis with aciclovir 400mg twice daily mandatory
Hepatitis B reactivation reported - screen all patients before treatment; monitor those with positive serology during and 6 months after treatment

indication: Daratumumab in combination with bortezomib and dexamethasone is recommended for use within the Cancer Drugs Fund as an option for treating relapsed multiple myeloma in people who have had 1 previous treatment. This weekly bortezomib regimen is for patients who experience neuropathy or those with pre-existing neuropathy.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Cycles 1-8 on 21-day cycle. Both twice-weekly and weekly regimens include 32 total bortezomib doses. Bortezomib given cycles 1 to 8 only (then cycle 9 onwards see separate protocol). Dose banded per national dose bands (2.5). Cycles 9 onwards: see separate protocol.

Cycles 1-3: days 1, 8, 15 SC. Cycles 4-8: day 1 only SC. Fixed dose 1800mg SC over 3-5 minutes into abdomen 7.5cm from navel. Rotate injection sites. Do not inject at other body sites. Continue until disease progression (cycle 9 onwards see separate protocol). For first dose: cannula should be inserted for emergency anaphylaxis treatment. Observe for 4 hours following first dose.

Cycles 1-3: 20mg days 1, 8, 15 (pre-daratumumab) and 20mg days 2, 9, 16 (post-daratumumab). Reduce to 10mg in over 75s. Cycles 4-8: 20mg day 1 (pre-daratumumab) and 20mg days 2, 8, 9, 15, 16 (reduce to 10mg in over 75s). Can be given as IV equivalent. Take in morning.

indication: Monotherapy for the treatment of adult patients with relapsed and refractory multiple myeloma, who have received at least three prior therapies, including an immunomodulatory agent, a proteasome inhibitor, and an anti-CD38 antibody and have demonstrated disease progression on the last therapy.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Step-up dose 1 (12mg): cycle 1 day 1 only. BCMA x CD3 bispecific T-cell engager antibody (ELREXFIO). Inject into subcutaneous tissue of abdomen (preferred) or thigh. Do not inject into red, bruised, tender or scarred skin. Minimum 2 days between step-up dose 1 and step-up dose 2. Pre-medications mandatory for first three doses. Tocilizumab must be available on ward before injection. Monitor for CRS and ICANS for 48 hours after step-up doses - patients must remain within close proximity of healthcare facility.
Step-up dose 2 (32mg): cycle 1 day 4 only. Minimum 3 days between step-up dose 2 and first full treatment dose. Pre-medications mandatory.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: weekly cycle 1; weekly cycles 2-6; biweekly cycle 7 onwards
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Full treatment dose 76mg. Cycle 1: days 8, 15, 22. Cycles 2-6: days 1, 8, 15, 22 (weekly). Cycle 7 onwards: days 1 and 15 (every 2 weeks). Total 16 cycles. Minimum 6 days between doses. 28-day cycle.

pre_medications.timing: 60 minutes before elranatamab injection (first 3 doses mandatory)
pre_medications.indication: Pre-medication for step-up doses and first full treatment dose

pre_medications.timing: 60 minutes before elranatamab injection (first 3 doses mandatory)
pre_medications.indication: Pre-medication for step-up doses and first full treatment dose. Can be given as 20mg IV bolus if patient has not taken oral.

pre_medications.timing: 60 minutes before elranatamab injection (first 3 doses mandatory)
pre_medications.indication: Pre-medication for step-up doses and first full treatment dose

pre_medications.timing: As required - must be available on ward before each elranatamab injection
pre_medications.indication: CRS management. Max dose 800mg. 8-hourly if required (max 3 doses per 6 weeks). One dose must be on ward prior to each injection during cycles 1 and 2.

pre_medications.timing: When required for injection related reactions
pre_medications.indication: Treatment of elranatamab injection related reactions

take_home_medicines.special_instructions: Tumour lysis prophylaxis, first cycle only.
VZV/HSV prophylaxis. All cycles.

take_home_medicines.special_instructions: PCP prophylaxis. All cycles.
Antifungal prophylaxis. All cycles.
For patients with additional VTE risk factors. Options: dalteparin 5000 units SC OD, enoxaparin 40mg SC OD, heparin 5000 units SC BD, apixaban 2.5mg oral BD. Aspirin may be considered after cycle 6.
All cycles.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelet count less than 25x10⁹/L OR platelets 25-50x10⁹/L with bleeding
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold treatment until platelet count ≥25x10⁹/L and no evidence of bleeding.
Withhold treatment until ANC ≥0.5x10⁹/L.
Withhold treatment until ANC ≥1x10⁹/L and fever resolves.
Withhold treatment until haemoglobin ≥8g/dL.
No dose adjustment required.
Insufficient data - no dose recommendation.

dose_modifications.condition: Mild or moderate renal impairment (eGFR ≥30 ml/min)
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment required. Insufficient data for severe renal impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: Last dose step-up dose 1 (12mg), delay ≤14 days
dose_modifications.notes: Restart at step-up dose 2 (32mg). If tolerated, increase to 76mg 4 days later.

dose_modifications.condition: Last dose step-up dose 1 (12mg), delay >14 days
dose_modifications.notes: Restart step-up dosing schedule at step-up dose 1 (12mg).

dose_modifications.condition: Last dose any full treatment dose (76mg), delay ≤42 days
dose_modifications.notes: Restart at 76mg.

dose_modifications.condition: Last dose any full treatment dose (76mg), delay 43-84 days
dose_modifications.notes: Restart at step-up dose 2 (32mg). If tolerated, increase to 76mg 1 week later.

dose_modifications.condition: Last dose any full treatment dose (76mg), delay >84 days
dose_modifications.notes: Restart step-up dosing schedule at step-up dose 1 (12mg).
Consider delaying treatment.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: To initiate new cycle: ANC ≥1.0x10⁹/L, Haemoglobin ≥80g/L, Platelets ≥50x10⁹/L.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs, U&Es, bone profile, CRP and LDH prior to day 1 of treatment
Documented viral screen: CMV, HSV, EBV, VZV, HIV, hepatitis B, hepatitis C (EBV and CMV PCRs)
2-4 weekly CMV and EBV monitoring
Urine protein/creatinine ratio every 3-4 weeks
Calcium and magnesium at regular intervals
Immunoglobulins and paraprotein monthly
VTE risk assessment prior to starting treatment
Hepatitis B status before starting
Temperature, blood pressure and oxygen saturation 4-hourly after elranatamab on Day 0 then twice daily
CRS graded on WBMT CRS Assessment Form
Monitor for CRS and ICANS for 48 hours after step-up doses
Patients hospitalised 7-10 days for step-up doses monitoring at many centres
special_warnings: Bispecific T-cell engager (BCMA x CD3) antibody - ELREXFIO brand
Step-up dosing mandatory: 12mg day 1, then 32mg day 4, then 76mg weekly from day 8 - to reduce CRS and ICANS
CRS monitoring mandatory: patients must remain near healthcare facility for 48 hours after each of the 2 step-up doses
Tocilizumab 8mg/kg (max 800mg) IV must be available on ward before every elranatamab injection during cycles 1 and 2
Patients who experienced Grade ≥2 CRS or received tocilizumab with most recent injection must be hospitalised for next injection
ICANS monitoring required - refer to Wessex guidelines on monitoring bispecific antibody therapy related CRS and neurological toxicity
This protocol may require funding approval
Pre-medications mandatory for first 3 doses (step-up dose 1, step-up dose 2, and first 76mg dose)
Consider pethidine 25-50mg IV for elranatamab-related rigors failing to respond to steroids
Viral screen mandatory before starting; 2-4 weekly CMV/EBV monitoring throughout
Thromboprophylaxis required for patients with additional VTE risk factors

drugs.special_instructions: Step-up dose 1 (12mg): cycle 1 day 1 only. BCMA x CD3 bispecific T-cell engager antibody (ELREXFIO). Inject into subcutaneous tissue of abdomen (preferred) or thigh. Do not inject into red, bruised, tender or scarred skin. Minimum 2 days between step-up dose 1 and step-up dose 2. Pre-medications mandatory for first three doses. Tocilizumab must be available on ward before injection. Monitor for CRS and ICANS for 48 hours after step-up doses - patients must remain within close proximity of healthcare facility.
drugs.notes: step-up dose 1 - cycle 1 day 1 only | Step-up dose 1 (12mg): cycle 1 day 1 only. BCMA x CD3 bispecific T-cell engager antibody (ELREXFIO). Inject into subcutaneous tissue of abdomen (preferred) or thigh. Do not inject into red, bruised, tender or scarred skin. Minimum 2 days between step-up dose 1 and step-up dose 2. Pre-medications mandatory for first three doses. Tocilizumab must be available on ward before injection. Monitor for CRS and ICANS for 48 hours after step-up doses - patients must remain within close proximity of healthcare facility.

drugs.special_instructions: Step-up dose 2 (32mg): cycle 1 day 4 only. Minimum 3 days between step-up dose 2 and first full treatment dose. Pre-medications mandatory.
drugs.notes: step-up dose 2 - cycle 1 day 4 only | Step-up dose 2 (32mg): cycle 1 day 4 only. Minimum 3 days between step-up dose 2 and first full treatment dose. Pre-medications mandatory.

drugs.frequency: weekly cycle 1; weekly cycles 2-6; biweekly cycle 7 onwards
drugs.special_instructions: Full treatment dose 76mg. Cycle 1: days 8, 15, 22. Cycles 2-6: days 1, 8, 15, 22 (weekly). Cycle 7 onwards: days 1 and 15 (every 2 weeks). Total 16 cycles. Minimum 6 days between doses. 28-day cycle.
drugs.notes: weekly cycle 1; weekly cycles 2-6; biweekly cycle 7 onwards | Full treatment dose 76mg. Cycle 1: days 8, 15, 22. Cycles 2-6: days 1, 8, 15, 22 (weekly). Cycle 7 onwards: days 1 and 15 (every 2 weeks). Total 16 cycles. Minimum 6 days between doses. 28-day cycle.

indication: Multiple myeloma in selected patients of good performance status with resistant disease, particularly where urgent disease control is required.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: IV injection over 5 seconds. Dose banded per agreed bands. IMPORTANT: dose of bortezomib in this protocol is higher than that used in other versions - confirm with consultant before prescribing. Days 8 and 11 may be given as outpatient.

10mg/m²/day. Continuous intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 24 hours or via portable infusion device. Dose banded. Exfoliant - extravasation risk. Consider carboplatin 50mg/m²/day x4 days if CrCl <40ml/min.

400mg/m²/day. Continuous intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 24 hours (mixed with cisplatin and etoposide). Dose banded. Neutral extravasation. Consider mesna in patients with pre-existing bladder disorders: 40% of cyclophosphamide dose (rounded to nearest 400mg) orally at 0, 2 and 6 hours after cyclophosphamide.

40mg/m²/day. Continuous intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 24 hours (mixed with cisplatin and cyclophosphamide). Dose banded. Irritant extravasation. Reduce dose if albumin significantly low.

10mg/m²/day. Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 24 hours or via portable infusion device (separate bag from PACE). Dose banded. VESICANT - extravasation risk. Maximum lifetime cumulative dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy). Discontinue if cardiac failure develops. Baseline LVEF required if cardiac history/risk factors.
40mg once a day in the morning days 1-4. Haematological modifications not necessary. Take with or after food.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: once a day at night, days 1-21
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Cycle 1: 50mg once a day at night, days 1-21 inclusive. Cycle 2-3: 100mg once a day at night (dose escalation optional - consult). Taken at night to avoid daytime drowsiness. TERATOGENIC - Pregnancy Prevention Programme mandatory for all. Available as 50mg capsules. Haematological modifications not necessary.

pre_medications.timing: 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy, then three times a day for 5 days
15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy, then twice a day for 5 days

take_home_medicines.special_instructions: TERATOGENIC. Pregnancy Prevention Programme. Short course oral chemotherapy - must not be continued beyond prescribed course. Prescription Authorisation Form required.
To be written on in-patient chart.
VZV/HSV prophylaxis.
Antibacterial prophylaxis.

take_home_medicines.frequency: once a day on Mon/Wed/Fri (or consider pentamidine nebules 300mg every 4 weeks)
take_home_medicines.special_instructions: PCP prophylaxis.
Antifungal prophylaxis.
If platelets >50x10⁹/L. Options: dalteparin 5000 units SC OD, enoxaparin 40mg SC OD, heparin 5000 units SC BD. DVT prophylaxis mandatory with thalidomide.
Cycle 1 only. Tumour lysis prophylaxis.

dose_modifications.notes: 100% dose. Haematological modifications not necessary for dexamethasone or thalidomide.
Consider delay until recovery. In the presence of cytopenias due to marrow involvement with myeloma, cycle 1 day 1 may proceed even if counts below threshold - confirm with consultant.
Initiate at 0.7mg/m². Escalate to 1mg/m² or reduce to 0.5mg/m² based on tolerability.
75% dose.
50% dose.
25% dose.
Omit doxorubicin.
Consider 50% dose reduction.
Clinical decision.
50% dose.
Omit cisplatin. Consider carboplatin 50mg/m²/day x4 days as alternative.
75% dose.
Omit cyclophosphamide.
75% dose.
50% dose.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 1 with pain or grade 2
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 1mg/m² or switch to weekly bortezomib 1.3mg/m².

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 2 with pain or grade 3
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue until grade 1 or less, reinitiate at 0.7mg/m².

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 4 or severe autonomic neuropathy
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue bortezomib.
Suspend treatment or reduce dose by at least 50%. Reintroduce at reduced dose if symptoms resolve to grade 1 or below.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above, or toxicity not resolving
dose_modifications.notes: Stop thalidomide.
Discontinue doxorubicin permanently.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L and platelets ≥75x10⁹/L for 100% dose. Consider delay if below thresholds. Cycle 1 day 1 may proceed with cytopenias due to myeloma marrow involvement - confirm with consultant.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es (including calcium and magnesium) prior to day 1
FBC assessed frequently as blood products often required
Regular blood glucose monitoring
Baseline LVEF before starting (cardiac history, risk factors or elderly); discontinue doxorubicin if cardiac failure
Paraprotein or light chains every 3 weeks

Pregnancy testing in women of childbearing potential: negative test within 3 days of starting thalidomide, repeat every 4 weeks (every 2 weeks if irregular menstrual cycles), final test 4 weeks after last thalidomide dose
special_warnings: IN-PATIENT REGIMEN - intensive 4-day continuous infusion regimen requiring hospitalisation
Central catheter or PICC line advised for administration
Multiple versions of this protocol exist - ensure correct version selected for diagnosis
BORTEZOMIB DOSE: this protocol uses 1.3mg/m² IV which is higher than some other versions - confirm with consultant
THALIDOMIDE IS HIGHLY TERATOGENIC: Pregnancy Prevention Programme mandatory for all patients and prescribers; Prescription Authorisation Form required
Thalidomide increases DVT/PE risk: thromboprophylaxis mandatory (LMWH preferred for first 5 months; therapeutic warfarin alternative)
Doxorubicin is a VESICANT - extravasation risk
Cisplatin is an EXFOLIANT - extravasation risk
Maximum lifetime cumulative doxorubicin dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² with prior mediastinal radiotherapy)
Growth factors recommended from day 5 until neutrophils >1x10⁹/L
Thalidomide dose escalates on cycle 2 (optional): 50mg cycle 1, 100mg cycles 2-3
VZV prophylaxis with aciclovir 400mg twice daily mandatory
Short course oral chemotherapy (thalidomide) - must not be continued beyond prescribed course; NPSA alert applies

ORAL CHEMOTHERAPY — NPSA/2008/RRR001: Patient must be counselled and provided written information before starting. Prescriptions must be written on an approved oral chemotherapy prescription form. Dispensing must only be by a trained pharmacist. Patient must be provided with a chemotherapy alert card. Do not supply more than one cycle at a time without clinical review.
